generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input electric current of the robot joint actuator"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentMeasurementTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring motor angular velocity of the robot joint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityMeasurementTransmitted
]

instance FirstAxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring first‑axis position of the robotic manipulator"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisPositionMeasurementTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance CurrentMeasurementTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityMeasurementTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance AxisPositionMeasurementTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionSensor
]

// C10: Data
instance CurrentMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered input current measurement"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentMeasurementTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingComponent, ModelUpdateEngine
]

instance AngularVelocityMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered motor angular velocity measurement"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityMeasurementTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingComponent, ModelUpdateEngine
]

instance AxisPositionMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered first‑axis position measurement"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisPositionMeasurementTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingComponent, ModelUpdateEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PreprocessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filter component that preprocesses raw sensor signals before MQTT publishing"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeJointFrictionMonitoringService, PredictiveFaultDetectionService
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Mosquitto MQTT broker converting measurements to JSON and publishing on hierarchical topics"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance AASExecutor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell executor that assembles the digital shadow and stores it in ThingsBoard"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
]

instance ModelUpdateEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine that retrains the neural‑network friction model and updates Simulink friction equations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelUpdateService
]

instance FrictionCompensationController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Control loop component that applies active friction compensation commands to the robot"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCompensationService
]

instance ParameterOptimizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine that runs simulations for machining parameter optimisation and quality prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables MachiningParameterOptimizationService, ProductQualityPredictionService
]

instance ThingsBoardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ThingsBoard instance that renders digital‑shadow data on dashboards"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeJointFrictionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of joint friction using sensor data and updated models"
    DTDFVocab:provides JointFrictionInsight
]

instance PredictiveFaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive fault detection based on friction trends and sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultPredictionInsight
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Transmission of preprocessed sensor data via MQTT to the AAS executor"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionInsight
]

instance ModelUpdateService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatic updating of physics‑based and neural‑network friction models in real time"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelUpdateAction
]

instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active compensation of joint friction through a closed‑loop controller"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation‑based optimisation of machining parameters to reduce CO₂ emissions"
    DTDFVocab:provides CO2EmissionReductionAction
]

instance ProductQualityPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Prediction of product quality based on integrated digital‑twin data"
    DTDFVocab:provides QualityPredictionInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualisation of digital‑shadow data on ThingsBoard"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Central data memory service storing the digital shadow in ThingsBoard"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance JointFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current joint friction levels enabling compensation"
]

instance FaultPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of imminent joint faults derived from friction anomalies"
]

instance DataTransmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confirmation that sensor data have been successfully transmitted to the digital twin"
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic updating of physics‑based and neural‑network friction models with new data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Active compensation of internal joint friction via the control loop"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CO2EmissionReductionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimisation of machining parameters to reduce CO₂ emissions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance QualityPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of product quality based on integrated sensor and model data"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualisation of digital‑shadow data on the ThingsBoard dashboard"
]

// C10: Models
instance PhysicsBasedFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White‑box analytical model of joint friction implemented in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelUpdateEngine
]

instance NeuralNetworkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black‑box neural‑network model for joint friction trained online"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelUpdateEngine
]

instance StateMachineControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State‑machine model governing the control loop for friction compensation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionCompensationController
]

instance ProcessingComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model of the processing component that consumes traces and invokes simulation/calculation models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingComponent
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin operates in real‑time, receiving a continuous data stream from the robot via MQTT; the processing (e.g., friction model simulation) must respect the control system’s dead‑time, i.e., latency lower than the control loop period, enabling sub‑cycle updates. Synchronization is therefore continuous with granularity at the control‑loop cycle level."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a pipeline architecture: sensor data are streamed over MQTT to ThingsBoard, the AAS Executor extracts measured, simulated, and reference values and triggers simulation models (SysML structural model and Simulink friction model via FMI). The resulting outputs are written back to the digital shadow in ThingsBoard and visualised on a dashboard. Orchestration and resource management are handled by the AAS‑based component that automatically builds, runs, and updates the digital shadow."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle follows the MBSE abstraction hierarchy described in Document 1: (1) requirements engineering, (2) functional architecture modeling, (3) logical architecture modeling, and (4) product/technical layer (physical implementation). Each phase is represented with a specific model language – SysML for requirements and functional views, ADL (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) for component‑connector structures, and simulation models (Simulink, DEVS) for behavior at the physical layer. Transition between phases is managed by traceability links defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Document 2) that associate data traces and processing components; conformance relations map concrete models to the reference model, providing governance of the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The twin’s fidelity is maintained by coupling model verification, transformation traceability and continuous validation against live sensor data. Model transformations (e.g., SysML → Simulink) are verified for correctness as required by the Digital Shadow methodology, ensuring that the analytical white‑box models preserve the semantics of the source architecture. Traceability links from each data trace to its originating processing component and underlying model allow systematic error detection and correction. Uncertainty arising from model approximations or sensor noise is mitigated by the adaptive update loop described for friction modelling: new measurements continuously recalibrate parameters of the white‑box simulation or retrain the black‑box neural network, thus quantifying and reducing prediction error. Quality‑assurance processes include comparison of simulated versus measured values, verification of model‑based transformations, and the use of metadata to record provenance and confidence levels for each data trace."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The system relies on a lightweight MQTT publish‑subscribe network (Mosquitto broker) with hierarchical topics that can be permission‑controlled for read/write access. Sensors publish JSON‑encoded measurement messages to the broker; the AAS Executor and ThingsBoard subscribe to the same topics. The network topology is essentially a star/central‑broker model linking the physical sensors, the preprocessing unit, the MQTT broker, the AAS Executor, and the ThingsBoard dashboard. No explicit bandwidth, latency, redundancy or security protocol details beyond MQTT’s standard TLS support are provided in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is realised through Industry 4.0 standards: MQTT for IoT data transport, ThingsBoard for storage and visualisation, the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for semantic wrapping, and the Functional Mock‑up Interface (FMI) to embed Simulink behavioural models with SysML structural models. These mechanisms provide interoperability with other enterprise systems and enable coupling with additional digital twins."
]
